headline: MyKad Biometric Data Can Be Cloned From Contactless Scans
context: Security researcher demonstrated MyKad biometric data cloneable from contactless scans raising identity theft concerns.

cards.big: Researcher shows MyKad biometric data can be cloned from contactless scans
cards.sub: Discovery raises identity theft concerns for all 28 million MyKad holders

cards.big: Vulnerability allows reading fingerprint templates from chip at 5cm distance
cards.sub: Researcher shared findings 6 months before disclosure without JPN response

cards.big: JPN confirmed vulnerability but said exploitation requires specialized equipment
cards.sub: Security community noted equipment costs only RM800 commercially available

cards.big: 28 million active MyKad use chip technology standardized in 2001 without updates
cards.sub: Next-generation MyKad planned for 2028 leaving 3-year exposure window

cards.big: MyKad vulnerability tests whether national identity infrastructure gets timely security updates
MyKad biometric data clonable from contactless scans. Identity documents must be secure against the attacks they face.
